title: Centella abbreviata
taxonomy: Centella abbreviata is a flowering plant in the family Hydrocotylaceae and is classified within the genus Centella. It is a perennial herb native to parts of Asia and Australia. The classification of Centella abbreviata is as follows: kingdom Plantae, clade Angiosperms, clade Eudicots, clade Asterids, and family Hydrocotylaceae. Centella abbreviata is closely related to other species within the genus, including Centella asiatica. Further classification of the species is pending ongoing research in the field of botany.

intro: Centella abbreviata, also known as small water hyssop, is a plant species within the family Apiaceae, native to tropical and subtropical regions. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and neuroprotective properties, which may aid in wound healing, improving cognitive function, and reducing stress. The bioactive constituents of Centella abbreviata include glycosides, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Centella abbreviata is commonly prepared as a tea or tincture, and is also used in topical applications for its purported benefits in skin care and wound healing.
